evidence.scientific_clarity: Stacked bars across 5 conditions with 4 classes each summing to 1 (100%) directly fulfills the brief; y-axis uses PercentFormatter 0–100%.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' both set; font family list leads with Arial (not DejaVu Sans default); base 8pt, ticks 7pt, legend 6.5pt are all legible at 3.5" width.
evidence.palette: No red/green directional colors used; however #0F4D92 (dark blue) and #42949E (teal) are both blue-family hues that may be indistinguishable under deuteranopia — not a certified colorblind-safe palette.
evidence.layout_density: Compact 3.5×2.8" figure with tight_layout; legend placed outside-right avoids overlap; y-range 0–1 is exact for proportions; no gaping whitespace.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption describes structure and colors but contains no specific numerical finding (e.g., 'Condition X has the highest Class A proportion at 45%'); a Reviewer 2 would request a concrete takeaway.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains /Subtype /CIDFontType2 with embedded ArialMT (GOFYPY+ArialMT), confirming real vector output with selectable/editable text; pdf.fonttype=42 explicitly set in script.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (18 KB), figure.png (35 KB), figure.svg (13 KB), caption.tex (397 B), gen_figure.py (2.1 KB) all present and non-trivial; data.json is an input not an output.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption correctly names classes, conditions, color mapping, and confirms bars sum to 1; lacks any quantitative finding or scientific claim that would make it stand alone.

overall_strengths: Proper vector/editable-text settings (pdf.fonttype=42, svg.fonttype='none') with ArialMT embedded in PDF — press-ready output.
Clean, compact layout (3.5×2.8") with correct y-range, percent axis labels, and outside-right legend that avoids bar overlap.
overall_weaknesses: Color palette pairs dark blue (#0F4D92) and teal (#42949E) which are not reliably distinguishable under common forms of color vision deficiency — should use a validated colorblind-safe set (e.g., Okabe-Ito or ColorBrewer qualitative).
Caption is purely descriptive with no numerical finding; a journal reviewer would require at least one concrete quantitative observation drawn from the data.
